enrichment.summary: Kyle gives a casual morning vlog update from Boulder, describing his previous day of working 9am to 2am on a Google Earth mapping project (Beaver Creek ski resort). He discusses his current productive headspace for tedious tracing/linking work, plans to tackle a 400-mountains-of-Boulder project, and mentions an interest in mapping unbuilt ski resorts and eventually using Boulder's buildings to recreate Rome in a project he calls 'sanctimonium.'
enrichment.speaker: kyle

stances.position: enjoys getting high in bed before starting the day, considers it part of his routine
believes in capitalizing on mental headspace when in the right mode for tedious/repetitive work
six hours feels sufficient when he wakes up fresh

transcript.text: Hello living in Boulder V-log. Just a second. I gotta remember to get my weed every time, man. I hit record and then all of a sudden I'm like, oh shit, the weed.
Alright, so, um, so yesterday what I did was, uh, I think I got up at like 9. I was working at like 9. And I worked all the way until 2 in the morning, solid.
Uh, cause, uh, I did Beaver Creek yesterday, finishing it up, and it's super complicated. Um, yeah. So I have to link it, finish linking it. I'm like halfway through linking it. I have like 100 more.
And then I'm gonna do Crested View, which is gonna be a lot easier probably, even though I have to add like a whole new section. Well, I had to at Beaver Creek too, I just didn't realize that.
Um, yeah. And then I was thinking about going back to Google Earth in Boulder. But I'm in a really good, uh, like, mode right now. In my head where I'm really good at, uh, the brain candy stuff.
Um, just listening to, I was listening to just all these different videos from history scientists over and over again. Just trying to memorize all the stuff that all the different emperors did.
And then listening to this other dude. And then, and then, uh, who does just the emperors. And then, uh, um, yeah. I was doing different chapters in the book. Theodosius, that era.
But, um, but yeah, I'm in a good mood, mental head space right now to just do that kind of work. And I have the 400 Mountains from Boulder.
Uh, it's, it's got a lot of work. Like, uh, two weeks or something. So maybe I'll jump into that for a little bit until I get bored with it.
It's good to, um, it's good to take advantage of when you're in a, when I roll, like, working with just the brain candy stuff.
Like the tedious stuff, you know. Repetitive motions.
Cutting and pasting and stuff. Uh, tracing a bunch of stuff. So, uh, that's what I'll do.
Like, I got really high before I did this video. Cause I woke up and then I got high in bed and I just lay there. I like to do that.
And then I got up. So I probably didn't need to get my weed.
Ooh. Um. Yeah, so that's what I'll do today.
Oh, but I wanted to do the ski resorts that were never built.
I think I said this yesterday. There's like five of them. And some of them are really big.
And then make my own. I haven't even looked at the mountains yet to see where I want to put it.
And then I would, I'd plug that into, um, uh, my sanctimonium project, my Rome.
If I build Boulder on Google Earth, I can then copy it.
And then use all those buildings to create Rome.
On Boulder without ever actually having to build, rebuild it. Cause it's already built.
I, uh, it's eight o'clock now.
So I only got six hours of sleep, but I'm fresh.
So getting a good start to the day.
